frameworks.name: bounded accumulation / drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: decision formation as accumulation of sensory evidence toward bounds

frameworks.name: starting-point bias account of choice history
frameworks.one_line: common assumption that history shifts the accumulator's starting point

frameworks.name: endogenous attention
frameworks.one_line: history-dependent shift of attention toward previously selected interpretation

core_question: Does choice history bias perceptual decisions by shifting the starting point of evidence accumulation or by changing the accumulation process itself?
key_assumptions: perceptual choices arise from bounded accumulation of sensory evidence
history biases can be captured by history-dependent parameters of accumulator models
individual differences in overt history bias reflect underlying model parameters
manipulations: task protocol across multiple perceptual choice paradigms
model variants differing in which parameter carries the history bias (starting point vs drift/accumulation)
key_findings: individual history biases were consistently explained by history-dependent change in evidence accumulation
history biases were not well explained by a shift in starting point
this pattern held across multiple task protocols and sensory modalities
interpretations: choice history signals bias interpretation of incoming sensory evidence rather than presetting the decision
the effect is akin to history-dependent shifting of endogenous attention toward (or away from) the prior choice
